mnemonics: Greedy Grab: Always grabs the node that looks closest to the goal, like a greedy kid grabbing the biggest cookie.
keyPoints: Uses a heuristic function (h(n)) to estimate the cost to the goal from node n.
It is not optimal; the shortest path might be overlooked for a seemingly closer goal.
It is not complete; it can get stuck in infinite loops if no cycle checking is used.
commonMistakes: Assuming it will always find the optimal (shortest/cheapest) path.
Confusing it with A* search, which uses both the cost from the start (g(n)) and the heuristic to the goal (h(n)).
